pi_items.source: Downloaded and verified file exists at this URL; matches official Wireshark SampleCaptures wiki
Confirmed in setup_task.sh as the filename used for the downloaded PCAP
Confirmed in both setup_task.sh and export_result.sh
tshark -r BITTORRENT.pcap -Y 'bittorrent' confirmed multiple BitTorrent handshake packets
tshark -r BITTORRENT.pcap -Y 'bittorrent.peer_id' -T fields -e bittorrent.peer_id; decodes to uTorrent client -UT1610-
tshark -r BITTORRENT.pcap -Y 'bittorrent.info_hash' -T fields -e ip.src; IP of first BitTorrent handshake initiator

pi_items.verified_value: libpcap (pcap capture file, microsecond ts, little-endian, version 2.4)
pi_items.source: file command output on downloaded BITTORRENT.pcap
Confirmed via Wireshark SampleCaptures wiki page at https://wiki.wireshark.org/SampleCaptures; file listed as 'Capture file of two torrent clients communicating without DHT or peer exchange'
tshark JSON output shows frame.time: 'Apr 11, 2007 12:51:36.683310000 EDT'

privileged_info_summary: The BITTORRENT.pcap file is from Wireshark's official SampleCaptures collection (not synthetic data). It contains captured BitTorrent traffic between two clients (10.10.10.23 and 10.10.10.22) from April 2007. The torrent's Info Hash (SHA1) is e21ea9569b69bab33c97851d0298bdfa89bc9092, the initiating client's Peer ID is 2d5554313631302dea812fcd6a3563e3be40c1d1 (uTorrent -UT1610- client), and its IP address is 10.10.10.23. The file can be accessed via the documented URL and contains 1,256 packets showing a complete BitTorrent handshake and data transfer exchange.
